# libraries
```{r}
library(microViz)
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(vegan)
library(data.table)
library(tidyverse)
```

# read phyloseq
```{r}
ps <- readRDS('/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v3.rds')
#ps <- readRDS('/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_dups_samples_batch_check_all_physeq_wgs.rds')
#ps <- readRDS('/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_RESEQUENCING_wgs.rds')
#ps@sam_data$Age = as.numeric(ps@sam_data$Age)
#ps@sam_data$Weight <- as.numeric(ps@sam_data$Weight)
#ps@sam_data$Height <- as.numeric(ps@sam_data$Height)
#saveRDS(ps, '/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v3.rds')
#meta <- read_csv('metadata_eng_prepared.csv')
ps <- ps
det <- 1/100
prev <- 10/100
level = 'Species'
taxas <- core_members(ps, detection = det, prevalence = prev, include.lowest = F)
ps <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps)
ps <- speedyseq::tax_glom(ps, taxrank = level, NArm = TRUE)
```
```{r}
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Define groups and remove bad samples
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
group_col <- "Batch"
ranks <- c("Family")

bad_batch_samples <- c('B-024-1', 'B-024-2', 'B-027-2', 'B-030-2')

if (length(bad_batch_samples) > 0) {
  ps_obj <- prune_samples(!(sample_names(ps) %in% bad_batch_samples), ps)
  cat("Removed", length(bad_batch_samples), "samples\n")
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Function to create a distinct colour palette
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
create_distinct_palette <- function(fill_levels) {
  n <- length(fill_levels)
  hues <- seq(0, 360, length.out = n + 1)[1:n]
  brightness <- rep(c(0.9, 0.6, 0.8, 0.5, 0.7), length.out = n)
  cols <- hsv(h = hues / 360, s = 1, v = brightness)
  names(cols) <- fill_levels
  return(cols)
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Loop over taxonomic ranks
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
for (rank in ranks) {
  ps_agg <- tax_glom(ps_obj, rank, NArm = TRUE)
  top_taxa <- names(sort(taxa_sums(ps_agg), decreasing = TRUE)[1:30])
  ps_top <- prune_taxa(top_taxa, ps_agg)
  
  # Create barplot list (each element is a ggplot)
  p_list <- comp_barplot(
    ps_top,
    tax_level = rank,
    n_taxa = 30,
    group_by = group_col,
    merge_other = TRUE,
    sample_order = "aitchison",
    bar_outline_colour = NA,
    bar_width = 0.9
  )
  
  # ---- Extract the actual fill levels from the first plot ----
  # This ensures we get the exact names used in the plot (e.g., "Other" or "other")
  first_plot_data <- p_list[[1]]$data
  # The fill aesthetic is mapped to the column named by 'rank' (e.g., "Family")
  fill_levels <- unique(first_plot_data[[rank]])
  # If "Other" is present, it will be included; if not, it's fine.
  # Sort to have a consistent order (optional)
  fill_levels <- sort(fill_levels)
  
  cat("Fill levels for this rank:", paste(fill_levels, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # Generate colours for these exact levels
  my_colors <- create_distinct_palette(fill_levels)
  
  # ---- Unified theme ----
  unified_theme <- theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 36, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 28, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 28),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 28),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm")
  )
  
  # Apply the theme to each plot (no fill scale yet)
  p_list <- lapply(p_list, function(p) {
    p + unified_theme
  })
  
  # ---- Combine plots ----
  combined_plot <- wrap_plots(p_list, ncol = length(p_list), guides = "collect") +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 36))
  
  # Flip coordinates and add legend customisation
  combined_plot <- combined_plot & coord_flip()
  combined_plot <- combined_plot & guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      ncol = 1,
      title.theme = element_text(size = 36, face = "bold"),
      label.theme = element_text(size = 36)
    )
  )
  
  # ---- NOW apply the colour scale to ALL subplots at once using & ----
  # This overrides the default scale cleanly and shows only ONE warning (if any)
  combined_plot <- combined_plot & scale_fill_manual(
    values = my_colors,
    breaks = fill_levels   # optional, ensures legend order matches
  )
  
  # (Optional) Suppress the "Scale for fill is already present" warning
  # by wrapping the assignment in suppressWarnings() if you prefer
  # combined_plot <- suppressWarnings(combined_plot & scale_fill_manual(...))
  
  # ---- Save and print ----
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("/NO_SGB_BATCH_counts_top_30_", tolower(rank), "_BIGFIONT.png"),
    plot = combined_plot,
    width = 36,
    height = 28,
    dpi = 300
  )
  
  print(combined_plot)
}
```
